q: In homeostasis which of the following is kept constant:
Regarding parasympathetic nervous system, all are true except;
o: B. It is Secreto- motor system
C. It is originated from Cranio- sacral regions.
D. It causes increase of sweating and vasodilatation of skeletal muscle.

q: Regarding noradrenaline, the incorrect statement is:
o: A. It is stored in granulated vesicles
B. It is inactivated mainly by active re-uptake
C. It stimulates both a and B receptors
D. It released at pre and post ganglionie sympathetic fibers

q: Vagus nerve is responsible for all of the following actions, except;
Autonomic ganglia is a collection of cell bodies of neurons inside the CNS.
Neurotransmitters are released by endocytosis.
Parasympathetic fibers of facial nerve relay at sphenopalatine and submandibular ganglia.
A drug which blocks beta adrenergic receptors is likely to cause:
o: A. Dilation of the bronchi in the respiratory tract
B. Dilation of skeletal muscle blood vessels in response to circulating adrenaline
C. No effect on the heart
D. A decrease in the force of myocardial contraction

q: The sympathetic supply is not responsible for:
o: C. Watery secretion of sweat glands

q: Drooping of the eyelid (ptosis) is caused by damage to:
o: B. The parasympathetic supply to the eye
C. The sympathetic supply to the eye

q: A lesion in the cervical sympathetic chain (Horner’s syndrome) causes:
o: B. Drooping of the upper eyelid (ptosis)
C. Vasoconstriction of skin blood vessels (leading to pallor)

q: The parasympathetic nervous system affects all of these organs except:
The cells is mainly dependent upon activity of: (Final 23 Q1)
Which of the following is an example of primary active transport?
o: A. Glucose uptake into intestinal epithelial cells by Na+-glucose co-transporter 1
B. Ca2+ sequestration in sarcoplasmic reticulum by Ca2+-ATPase
C. Ca2+ extrusion from cells by the Na+-Ca2+ exchanger
D. H2O flux across cell membrane through aquaporin

q: Acetylcholine all are true except:
o: A. Acts on the same type of receptor in sympathetic and parasympathetic ganglia
B. Acts on the same receptors at sympathetic and parasympathetic nerve endings
C. Acts on the same receptors at autonomic ganglia and autonomic nerve terminals

q: In the parasympathetic system:
o: A. Axons of the postganglionic neurons are shorter than those of preganglionic
B. Preganglionic neurons are more numerous than the postganglionic neurons
C. Choline acetylase is the transmitter between the preganglionic and postganglionic neurons

q: Sympathetic stimulation of the iris causes:
The following about sympathetic nervous system is true except:
o: A. All its preganglionic fibres are cholinergic
B. It is involved in stress & emergency conditions
C. It has a vasodilatory effect on skeletal muscle blood vessels during rest
D. Its stimulation increases the cardiac properties

q: Beta adrenergic receptor stimulation does not cause:
Stimulation of the parasympathetic nervous system leads to:
o: D. Increased blood flow in coronary vessels

q: Muscarinic cholinergic receptors are found:
The termination of acetylcholine action is mainly due to:
o: B. Irreversible combination with cholinergic receptors

q: Sympathetic stimulation of the iris causes (Final 24 Q5):
A lesion in the cervical sympathetic chain (Horner’s syndrome) causes (Final 24 Q6):
o: B. Drooping of the upper eyelid (ptosis)
C. Vasoconstriction of the skin blood vessels (leading to pallor)

q: Collateral ganglia of the sympathetic nervous system include the:
Tachycardia due to sympathetic stimulation is mediated by:
o: C. Both Alpha and Beta receptors

q: Blocking of the nicotinic ganglionic receptors in the ANS supplying the heart leads to:
o: A. Inhibition of the sympathetic but not the parasympathetic
B. Inhibition of both sympathetic and parasympathetic
C. Decrease in the heart rate
D. Decrease in the force of contraction

q: The action of acetylcholine on its receptors is terminated mainly by:
o: B. Reuptake into the terminal buttons

q: Sympathetic stimulation is expected to lead to:
Which of the following best describes autonomic innervation of the heart?
o: A. The heart is only innervated by the sympathetic nervous system
B. The heart is only innervated by the parasympathetic nervous system
C. The heart is innervated by both and they have complementary effects
D. The heart is innervated by both and they have opposite effects

q: Muscarinic effects of acetylcholine can be inhibited by:
Regarding parasympathetic system:
o: A. It has mainly very short preganglionic fibers
B. Dopamine is released from its post-ganglionic nerve fiber
C. It stimulates movements and secretions of the GIT
D. It increases heart rate during exercise

q: Acetylcholinesterase:
o: B. Is the acetylcholine receptor in muscle tissue
C. Degrades and terminates the action of acetylcholine

q: Generalized sympathetic stimulation causes all the following except:
Among the manifestations of Horner’s syndrome is:
o: B. Drooping of the upper eyelid
D. Vasoconstriction of skin blood vessels

q: Beta-1 adrenergic receptor stimulation causes:
o: B. An increase in the heart rate

q: Atropine:
Concerning the autonomic ganglia, all the following is true EXCEPT:
o: A. They represent the relay site of preganglionic to postganglionic neurons
B. They are sympathetic and parasympathetic
C. Their chemical transmitter is acetylcholine

q: Which transmitter will result in dilatation of the pupil?
Noradrenaline is mainly removed by:
o: C. Active reuptake by nerve terminal

q: Acetylcholine is formed in all the following sites EXCEPT:
o: A. End of all postganglionic sympathetic fibers
C. Motor end plate of skeletal muscle
D. End of all postganglionic parasympathetic fibers

q: Stimulation of the vagus nerve leads to:
o: B. Vasoconstriction of coronary blood vessels

q: Sympathetic effect on the eye:
o: B. Increase the power of lens

q: Regarding the parasympathetic division of the autonomic nervous system, which statement is correct?
o: A. Parasympathetic preganglionic neurons are found in all cranial nerves
C. Parasympathetic postganglionic neurons are found in spinal segments from T1 to L2
D. Parasympathetic postganglionic neurons secrete acetylcholine onto their target organs

q: Stimulation of the sympathetic system causes:
Acetylcholine is secreted at all the following sites EXCEPT:
o: A. Postganglionic sympathetic nerve terminals to blood vessels of skeletal muscles
C. Preganglionic sympathetic & parasympathetic nerve terminals
D. Postganglionic sympathetic noradrenergic nerve terminals

q: The alpha adrenergic receptors mediate all the following effects EXCEPT:
o: C. Relaxation of the intestinal wall

q: Parasympathetic preganglionic neurons:
o: A. Are much shorter than the postganglionic neurons
B. Secrete the same transmitter as the postganglionic neurons
C. Originate from all the sacral segments of the spinal cord
D. There are some fibers to the ventricles of the heart

q: Inhibitors of choline esterase enzymes:
o: A. Enhance the effects of parasympathetic stimulation
B. Inhibit the effect of parasympathetic stimulation
C. Block the action of acetylcholine in autonomic ganglia
D. A and C are correct

q: Double innervation of viscera means:
o: A. Visceral organs receive both preganglionic and postganglionic autonomic nerves
B. Visceral organs receive both autonomic and somatic nerves
C. The sympathetic and parasympathetic nerves to viscera produce the same effect
D. Several visceral organs are innervated from both divisions of the autonomic nervous system

q: Regarding autonomic ganglia:
o: A. Paravertebral sympathetic ganglia are located on the sides of the vertebral column
B. Parasympathetic ganglia lie far away from the organ which they innervate
C. All fibers passing through these ganglia should relay in them
D. Adrenal medulla is a modified parasympathetic ganglion

q: Which of the following is NOT a symptom of Horner’s syndrome?
o: D. Vasodilatation of skin blood vessels

q: Which autonomic receptor mediates an increase in heart rate?
Parasympathetic nerves:
o: A. Have opposite effects to sympathetic nerves on the intestine
B. Have opposite effects to sympathetic nerves on salivary glands
C. Have longer postganglionic fibers than preganglionic fibers
D. None of the above is true

q: Alpha (a) adrenoceptors:
o: A. Can be stimulated by both adrenaline and noradrenaline
B. Are involved in heart rate response to noradrenaline

q: Generalized sympathetic activity is characterized by:
Stimulation of the vagus nerve causes:
o: A. Reduction in strength of ventricular contraction
B. Secretion & vasodilatation in salivary glands

q: Acetylcholine (Final 27 Thani):
o: A. Acts on same receptor type in sympathetic & parasympathetic ganglia
C. Is secreted by cholinergic fibers

q: Atropine (blocks muscarinic receptors) causes:
o: A. Paralysis of accommodation for near vision

q: Which of the following statements regarding the role of autonomic innervation are correct?
o: A. Stimulation of sympathetic nerves to the eyes causes dilation of the pupils
B. Activation of the sympathetic system causes vasodilatation in the skin
C. Stimulation of the vagus nerves speeds up the heart
D. Autonomic ganglia is the mother neuron of preganglionic fiber

q: Ptosis results from:
Regarding the sympathetic division of the autonomic nervous system:
o: A. Acetylcholine is secreted by some sympathetic postganglionic neurons
B. Sympathetic preganglionic neurons secrete noradrenaline (norepinephrine)
C. Sympathetic postganglionic neurons originate from spinal segments T1–L2
D. The sympathetic chain extends from the thoracic to sacral regions of the spinal cord
